message:
chore(beads): flush post-fuzz-expansion task updates

Single-line br sync flush carrying forward the task tracker state
after the fuzz-expansion + xattr + harness work in the previous
commit cluster. No code change.

 {"id":"bd-lqwc","title":"Add E2E test suite for graceful degradation under load","description":"# Add E2E test suite for graceful degradation under load

## GOAL
Validate the graceful degradation system works correctly under real system pressure.

## BACKGROUND
The Graceful Degradation system (bd-3tz) must:
- Detect system pressure correctly
- Transition between degradation levels
- Recover when pressure relieved
- Never cause data loss

## TEST SCRIPT: scripts/e2e/ffs_degradation_stress.sh

### 1. CPU Pressure Tests
```bash
test_cpu_pressure_degradation() {
    ffs mount "$IMAGE" "$MNT" --rw &
    FFS_PID=$!
    
    # Start with baseline measurements
    baseline_latency=$(measure_read_latency "$MNT")
    
    # Apply CPU pressure (16 stress processes)
    stress-ng --cpu 16 --timeout 30s &
    STRESS_PID=$!
    
    sleep 5
    
    # Measure degraded performance
    stressed_latency=$(measure_read_latency "$MNT")
    
    # Reads should still work (degraded but functional)
    assert_reads_work "$MNT"
    
    # Degradation level should have increased
    level=$(get_degradation_level_from_tui)
    assert_gte "$level" 1
    
    # Kill stress
    kill $STRESS_PID
    wait $STRESS_PID 2>/dev/null
    
    sleep 5
    
    # Should recover to normal
    recovered_latency=$(measure_read_latency "$MNT")
    assert_within_factor "$recovered_latency" "$baseline_latency" 2
    
    kill $FFS_PID
}
```

### 2. Memory Pressure Tests
```bash
test_memory_pressure_cache_reduction() {
    ffs mount "$IMAGE" "$MNT" --rw --cache-size 256M &
    FFS_PID=$!
    
    # Fill cache
    read_many_files "$MNT"
    initial_cache_size=$(get_cache_size)
    
    # Apply memory pressure
    stress-ng --vm 4 --vm-bytes 80% --timeout 30s &
    STRESS_PID=$!
    
    sleep 5
    
    # Cache should have reduced
    pressured_cache_size=$(get_cache_size)
    assert_lt "$pressured_cache_size" "$initial_cache_size"
    
    # Reads should still work
    assert_reads_work "$MNT"
    
    # OOM killer should NOT be invoked
    assert_no_oom
    
    kill $STRESS_PID
    wait $STRESS_PID 2>/dev/null
    
    kill $FFS_PID
}
```

### 3. Degradation Level Progression
```bash
test_degradation_level_progression() {
    ffs mount "$IMAGE" "$MNT" --rw &
    FFS_PID=$!
    
    # Level 0: Normal
    assert_level 0
    assert_scrub_running
    assert_gc_running
    
    # Level 1: Background paused
    apply_light_pressure
    wait_for_level 1
    assert_scrub_paused
    
    # Level 2: Cache reduced
    apply_medium_pressure
    wait_for_level 2
    assert_cache_reduced
    
    # Level 3: Writes throttled
    apply_heavy_pressure
    wait_for_level 3
    assert_writes_throttled
    
    # Level 4: Read-only
    apply_extreme_pressure
    wait_for_level 4
    assert_writes_rejected_erofs
    assert_reads_work
    
    # Recovery
    relieve_all_pressure
    wait_for_level 0
    assert_normal_operation
    
    kill $FFS_PID
}
```

### 4. No Data Loss Under Pressure
```bash
test_no_data_loss_under_pressure() {
    ffs mount "$IMAGE" "$MNT" --rw &
    FFS_PID=$!
    
    # Start continuous writes
    continuous_write "$MNT/datafile" &
    WRITE_PID=$!
    
    # Apply varying pressure
    for pressure_level in light medium heavy; do
        apply_pressure "$pressure_level"
        sleep 5
    done
    
    # Stop writes
    kill $WRITE_PID
    wait $WRITE_PID 2>/dev/null
    
    # Sync and unmount
    sync
    kill $FFS_PID
    wait $FFS_PID
    
    # Verify all acknowledged writes persisted
    ffs mount "$IMAGE" "$MNT" --ro
    verify_data_integrity "$MNT/datafile"
}
```

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Log degradation level transitions with timestamp
- Log pressure metrics (CPU, memory, IO)
- Log cache size changes
- Log background task state (scrub, GC)

## FIXTURES
- Use stress-ng for reproducible pressure
- Use cgroups for memory limits if available
- 256MB test image

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Degradation levels transition correctly
2. Recovery works when pressure relieved
3. No data loss at any degradation level
4. No OOM killer invocation
5. Read latency <10x baseline at Level 3","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:13:11.849026407Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T15:44:05.697170888Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T15:44:05.697147454Z","close_reason":"Completed: added scripts/e2e/ffs_degradation_stress.sh, updated scripts/e2e/README.md, and validated via full script run (PASS)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["degradation","e2e","harness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lqwc","depends_on_id":"bd-1bx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:13:21.511279429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lqwc","depends_on_id":"bd-2jk.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:49.307234125Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lqwc","depends_on_id":"bd-2r8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:13:21.414406994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lqwc","depends_on_id":"bd-3rix","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:02.733780633Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lqwc","depends_on_id":"bd-3tz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:38.869347159Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":90,"issue_id":"bd-lqwc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW: At 175 lines, this is the largest open bead. The 4 test sections (CPU pressure, memory pressure, degradation progression, no-data-loss) are coherent as a single E2E suite. No split needed, but if implementation reveals further complexity, consider splitting CPU/memory pressure into separate beads.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:04:50Z"},{"id":186,"issue_id":"bd-lqwc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: quality_assurance
Mapped graveyard sections: 0.1 Optimization Loop + 0.7 Artifact Contract + 6.12 Proptest
EV score: 50 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: A
Budgeted mode: bench_runs>=10, sample_floor=30 for noisy paths
Fallback trigger: golden-only gate, disable adaptive controller deployment

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:29Z"}]}
